existing_claim: §2.4 states as a committed decision: 'A design that relied on a lesson which later resolves, or on a promoted fact which is later retracted, must remain answerable by traversal to the fact as it stood when it was consumed.' §5.3 then concedes this guarantee 'is currently scoped to promoted facts' and that a decision consuming an un-promoted fact 'could age out the graph-native trace.' The decision as stated in §2.4 claims a survival guarantee broader than the mechanism the record acknowledges exists — the prose commits a consequence the substrate does not yet support.
incoming_claim: §2.4 states as a committed decision: 'A design that relied on a lesson which later resolves, or on a promoted fact which is later retracted, must remain answerable by traversal to the fact as it stood when it was consumed.' §5.3 then concedes 'The frozen-provenance survival guarantee is currently scoped to promoted facts; a design decision that consumed a fact without promoting it relies on Reasoning-Graph evidence under bounded retention, which could age out the graph-native trace.' The §2.4 prose commits a survival consequence broader than the mechanism the record's own Risks section acknowledges exists — the decided consequence outruns the declared substrate for the un-promoted-consumed case.

existing_claim: Tested whether the record over-claims its scope onto Reasoning-Graph capture — which would collide with ADR-001 §2.2's reasoning-capture invariant and ADR-002 §2.6's write-authority assignment, both of which govern RG capture as captured judgment, not ground truth. §2.1 explicitly bounds the invariant to 'KG authoritative ground truth' and states 'Reasoning-Graph capture is not ground truth ... and is outside this record's scope,' citing the correct owning authorities. The scope claim is fidelity-clean: the record decides only ground-truth mutation and does not reach into the RG-capture domain it names as another authority's.
incoming_claim: Attacked whether the record over-claims its scope onto Reasoning-Graph capture — which would reach into a domain ADR-001 §2.2 and ADR-002 §2.6 own, and would collide with the distillation control it does govern. §2.1 bounds the invariant to 'KG authoritative ground truth' and states 'Reasoning-Graph capture is not ground truth — it is captured judgment, governed by the reasoning-capture invariant and its write-authority assignment (ADR-001 §2.2; ADR-002 §2.6) — and is outside this record's scope.' The distillation control it commits (§2.2) targets a derived ObservedPattern entering the Operational KG plane, not RG capture, so no exemption gap opens between the two. The scope claim is fidelity-clean and decides exactly ground-truth mutation.

existing_claim: §2.4 states as a committed decision: 'A design that relied on a lesson which later resolves, or on a promoted fact which is later retracted, must remain answerable by traversal to the fact as it stood when it was consumed.' §5.3 then concedes this guarantee 'is currently scoped to promoted facts' and that a decision consuming an un-promoted fact 'could age out the graph-native trace.' The decision as stated in §2.4 claims a survival guarantee broader than the mechanism the record acknowledges exists — the prose commits a consequence the substrate does not yet support.
incoming_claim: §2.4 commits as a decision: 'A decision that relied on a promoted fact which is later retracted must remain answerable by traversal to the fact as it stood when it was consumed.' §2.4 immediately qualifies that the frozen-provenance survival guarantee is scoped to promoted facts, and §5.3 concedes a design consuming an un-promoted fact 'relies on Reasoning-Graph evidence under bounded retention, which could age out the graph-native trace.' The §2.4 survival requirement is stated as a committed platform property broader than any built mechanism honors for the un-promoted-consumption case — the decided consequence outruns the substrate the record itself acknowledges, and whether the ADR should commit-broad-with-caveat versus commit-scoped-to-supported is resolved in one direction without reconciling the §2.4 requirement statement and the §5.3 concession into a single coherent commitment.

existing_claim: §2.4 commits, as a standing decision, that 'A design that relied on a lesson which later resolves, or on a promoted fact which is later retracted, must remain answerable by traversal to the fact as it stood when it was consumed.' §5.3 then concedes the survival guarantee 'is currently scoped to promoted facts' and that a decision consuming an un-promoted fact 'could age out the graph-native trace.' At the posture altitude, the §2.4 commitment stands ahead of the substrate that supports it: the record commits a survival requirement broader than any built mechanism honors and defers the extension to a follow-up. Whether an ADR should commit a requirement its own Risks section flags as unmet by present mechanism — versus committing the requirement scoped to what the substrate supports today — is a posture question the record resolves in one direction (commit-broad) without reconciling the two sections.
incoming_claim: §2.4 commits, as a standing decision, that 'A design that relied on a promoted fact which is later retracted must remain answerable by traversal to the fact as it stood when it was consumed.' §5.3 then concedes the frozen-provenance survival guarantee 'is currently scoped to promoted facts' and that a decision consuming an un-promoted fact 'relies on Reasoning-Graph evidence under bounded retention, which could age out the graph-native trace.' As a posture judgment: the record commits a survival requirement broader than any built mechanism honors and defers the extension to a follow-up. This is a hard-to-reverse commitment (a stated platform requirement) whose supporting substrate the record's own Risks section flags as unmet for the un-promoted-consumed case; whether the requirement should be committed broad-and-deferred or scoped to what the substrate supports today is a posture question the record resolves in one direction without reconciling the two sections. The deliberation caveat itself frames this as 'ADR commits the survival requirement; extending the mechanism → named DDR-002 + DDR-003 follow-up' — a forward dependency the §2.4 prose does not surface.

existing_claim: Attacked whether the record creates a dependency inversion by leaning on the forthcoming governance design — i.e. listing DDR-003 as an upstream it depends on, which would make the ADR non-standalone. The attack failed: §7 states 'This ADR is its upstream authority' and places the pointer-resolution obligation in the downstream record's definition of done, and §2.6 routes remedy-boundary and policy downstream without depending on them; the ADR never lists the forthcoming governance design as an upstream dependency. The citation direction is proportionate and one-way, consistent with the standalone-record posture. Held.
incoming_claim: Attacked whether the record creates a dependency inversion by leaning on the forthcoming governance design — listing it as an upstream this ADR depends on, which would make the standalone ADR non-standalone and pull against the ratified posture. The attack failed: §7 states 'This ADR is its upstream authority' and places the pointer-resolution obligation in the downstream record's definition of done; §2.6 routes remedy-boundary and policy downstream without depending on them; and the ADR nowhere lists the forthcoming governance design as an upstream dependency. The citation direction is one-way and proportionate — downstream records cite ADR-008, never the reverse — consistent with the standalone-record posture. Held.

existing_claim: §2.4 commits as a decision: 'A design that relied on a lesson which later resolves, or on a promoted fact which is later retracted, must remain answerable by traversal to the fact as it stood when it was consumed.' §5.3 then concedes 'The frozen-provenance survival guarantee is currently scoped to *promoted* facts; a design decision that consumed a fact without promoting it relies on Reasoning-Graph evidence under bounded retention, which could age out the graph-native trace.' The §2.4 survival commitment is broader than the mechanism §5.3 acknowledges exists, so the decision prose commits a consequence the substrate does not yet support for the un-promoted-consumption case.
incoming_claim: §2.4 commits as a standing decision: 'A design that relied on a promoted fact which is later retracted must remain answerable by traversal to the fact as it stood when it was consumed; the frozen-provenance survival guarantee is currently scoped to promoted facts, so a design that consumed a fact without promoting it relies on Reasoning-Graph evidence under bounded retention.' §5.3 concedes: 'The frozen-provenance survival guarantee is currently scoped to promoted facts; a design decision that consumed a fact without promoting it relies on Reasoning-Graph evidence under bounded retention, which could age out the graph-native trace.' The §2.4 statement of what 'must' survive is broader than the built mechanism §5.3 acknowledges exists for the un-promoted-consumption case — the decided requirement outruns the declared substrate.
